title: RAII Counter with Constructor and Destructor
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete a `Tracker` class whose constructor prints `"open <id>"` and destructor prints `"close <id>"`. Then trace the exact construction/destruction order for two objects.

## Input
Line 1: two integers `a` and `b` — the IDs for the two `Tracker` objects.

## Output
Four lines, in the exact order they occur:
- Construction of first object: `open <a>`
- Construction of second object: `open <b>`
- Destruction of second object: `close <b>`
- Destruction of first object: `close <a>`

## Requirements
1. `Tracker(int id)` — constructor stores `id` and prints `open <id>` followed by a newline.
2. `~Tracker()` — destructor prints `close <id>` followed by a newline.
3. In `main`, declare `Tracker t1{a}` then `Tracker t2{b}` in the same scope.
4. Do not call the destructor explicitly.
5. Hidden tests use IDs 0 and 99.

title: Complete the Destructor for a Scoped Logger
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the `~ScopedLog()` destructor so it prints the closing message when the logger goes out of scope.

## Input
Line 1: a string `name` (one word, no spaces).

## Output
Three lines:
- Line 1: `[<name>] start` (from constructor)
- Line 2: `[<name>] working` (from `main`)
- Line 3: `[<name>] end` (from destructor, when logger goes out of scope)

## Requirements
1. The constructor `ScopedLog(const std::string& name)` stores the name and prints `[<name>] start`.
2. Complete `~ScopedLog()` so it prints `[<name>] end`.
3. Do not modify the constructor or `main`.
4. Hidden tests include names with numbers like `task42`.
